text: Chapter 12 teaches seven consecutive eighth-note triplets through exercises anchored to Tune 5 (It Happened), progressing from structured patterns to randomized triplet practice. The chapter introduces polymetric layering through a 5/4 over 4/4 rhythm exercise, requiring the player to feel conflicting metric frameworks simultaneously. The characteristic move is pedagogical progression from tune-bound patterns to improvisational randomization, elevating mechanical facility into spontaneous application. Load-bearing terminology includes triplet as rhythmic unit, metric superimposition, and randomized exercise as a tool for internalizing rhythm rather than reciting it.
